title: wangsania insolens
side_effects: Wangsania insolens can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal issues. Ingestion of the plant can cause vomiting, diarrhea, and stomach cramps, while topical application can lead to redness, itching, and blistering of the skin. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can cause kidney damage and affect blood pressure, making it crucial to handle it with caution. Improper use of the Wangsania insolens plant can also lead to respiratory problems, including coughing and shortness of breath.
